available_ingredients: Rice, Lentils, Turmeric, Cumin, Garlic, Salt, Mustard Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice - 1 cup, Lentils - ½ cup, Turmeric - ½ tsp, Cumin - 1 tsp, Garlic - 4 cloves, Salt - to taste, Mustard Oil - 1 tbsp
instructions: Boil lentils with turmeric and salt. In a separate pan, heat mustard oil, add garlic and cumin seeds. Pour this tempering over the cooked lentils. Serve with steamed rice for a balanced meal.

available_ingredients: Spinach, Mustard Greens, Garlic, Ginger, Salt, Mustard Oil
recipe_ingredients: Spinach - 1 bunch, Mustard Greens - 1 bunch, Garlic - 4 cloves, Ginger - 1 inch, Salt - to taste, Mustard Oil - 1 tbsp
instructions: Heat mustard oil, add garlic and ginger. Sauté for a minute, then add the chopped greens. Cook on low heat until tender. Serve as a side dish.

recipe_ingredients: Buckwheat Flour - 1 cup, Water - ½ cup, Salt - to taste, Ghee - 1 tbsp
instructions: Mix buckwheat flour with water and salt to form a dough. Flatten into round shapes and cook on a griddle with ghee until golden brown. Serve with a light vegetable curry.

available_ingredients: Rice, Fermented Bamboo Shoots, Tomatoes, Salt, Mustard Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice - 1 cup, Fermented Bamboo Shoots - ½ cup, Tomatoes - 2, Salt - to taste, Mustard Oil - 1 tbsp
instructions: Heat mustard oil, add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add bamboo shoots, salt, and water. Simmer until the bamboo is tender. Serve with rice.

recipe_ingredients: Millet Flour - 1 cup, Water - 3 cups, Buttermilk - ½ cup, Salt - to taste
instructions: Soak millet flour overnight. Boil water and add the millet flour, stirring to avoid lumps. Add buttermilk and salt for a refreshing and nourishing drink.

available_ingredients: Rice, Soybeans, Garlic, Ginger, Salt, Mustard Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice - 1 cup, Soybeans - ½ cup (fermented), Garlic - 4 cloves, Ginger - 1 inch, Salt - to taste, Mustard Oil - 1 tbsp
instructions: Heat mustard oil, add garlic and ginger. Add soybeans and water, cooking until tender. Serve as a protein-rich curry with rice.

available_ingredients: Rice, Vegetables (Spinach, Carrot, Beans), Ginger, Garlic, Salt, Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice - 1 cup, Spinach - 1 bunch, Carrot - 1, Beans - 10, Ginger - 1 inch, Garlic - 4 cloves, Salt - to taste, Oil - 1 tbsp
instructions: Boil rice noodles. Heat oil, sauté garlic and ginger, add chopped vegetables, and cook until tender. Add water and noodles. Simmer for a wholesome soup.

available_ingredients: Rice, Lentils, Mustard Oil, Salt, Turmeric, Garlic, Ginger
recipe_ingredients: Rice - 1 cup, Lentils - ½ cup, Mustard Oil - 1 tbsp, Salt - to taste, Turmeric - ½ tsp, Garlic - 4 cloves, Ginger - 1 inch
instructions: Cook lentils with turmeric and salt. Heat mustard oil, add garlic and ginger. Mix with the cooked lentils. Serve with rice for a balanced, fiber-rich meal.

recipe_ingredients: Rice Flour - 1 cup, Yogurt - ½ cup, Salt - to taste, Water - 2 cups
instructions: Boil water and add rice flour, stirring continuously. Add yogurt and salt. Cook until thickened. This fermented dairy dish is great for gut health.

available_ingredients: Rice, Black Lentils, Garlic, Ginger, Mustard Oil, Salt
recipe_ingredients: Rice - 1 cup, Black Lentils - ½ cup, Garlic - 5 cloves, Ginger - 1 inch, Mustard Oil - 1 tbsp, Salt - to taste
instructions: Boil lentils with salt and turmeric. In a separate pan, heat mustard oil, sauté garlic and ginger. Mix the lentils and serve with rice.

available_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Black Gram Lentils, Ginger, Garlic, Cumin, Salt, Mustard Oil
recipe_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Black Gram Lentils, Ginger, Garlic, Cumin, Salt, Mustard Oil
instructions: Soak the black gram lentils overnight, then grind them into a coarse paste. Mix the paste with chopped garlic, grated ginger, cumin seeds, and salt. Knead wheat flour with water and a pinch of salt to form a soft dough. Roll out small discs, fill them with the lentil mixture, and seal the edges. Steam the stuffed discs for 20-25 minutes. Drizzle with mustard oil and serve hot.

available_ingredients: Chickpeas, Buttermilk, Mustard Seeds, Turmeric, Green Chilies, Salt
recipe_ingredients: Chickpeas, Buttermilk, Mustard Seeds, Turmeric, Green Chilies, Salt
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight and cook them until tender. Whisk buttermilk with turmeric and salt. Heat mustard oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, and let them crackle. Add green chilies and sauté. Slowly pour the buttermilk into the pan, stirring continuously. Add the cooked chickpeas and let it simmer until thick. Serve with rice.

available_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Spinach, Green Chilies, Salt, Ghee
recipe_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Spinach, Green Chilies, Salt, Ghee
instructions: Finely chop the spinach and mix it with salt and green chilies to make a filling. Knead wheat flour with water into a firm dough. Divide the dough into balls, roll into discs, and fill with the spinach mixture. Fold and seal the edges. Flatten and cook on a griddle with ghee until golden brown. Serve with curd or pickle.

available_ingredients: Black Lentils, Wheat Flour, Yogurt, Garlic, Ginger, Salt, Mustard Oil
recipe_ingredients: Trout Fish, Black Lentils, Wheat Flour, Yogurt, Garlic, Ginger, Salt, Mustard Oil
instructions: Clean the trout fish and marinate it with yogurt, garlic paste, ginger, and salt. Heat mustard oil in a pan and shallow fry the fish until golden. Boil black lentils and mash slightly. Serve the fried fish with the lentils and a side of steamed rice.

recipe_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Jaggery, Ghee
instructions: Mix wheat flour and water to form a smooth batter. Add melted jaggery to the batter. Heat ghee in a pan and pour in the batter. Cook until both sides are golden brown. Serve as a dessert or snack.

available_ingredients: Rice, Yogurt, Cumin, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves, Salt
recipe_ingredients: Rice, Yogurt, Cumin, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves, Salt
instructions: Cook rice and let it cool. Whisk yogurt and add it to the rice. Heat oil in a small pan, add mustard seeds, cumin, and curry leaves for tempering. Pour the tempering over the rice and mix well. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

available_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Spinach, Ginger, Garlic, Mustard Oil, Salt
recipe_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Spinach, Ginger, Garlic, Mustard Oil, Salt
instructions: Prepare a thin batter using wheat flour, water, and salt. Add finely chopped spinach, ginger, and garlic to the batter. Heat a griddle, pour the batter to form a thin pancake, and cook on both sides until crispy. Serve with chutney or yogurt.

available_ingredients: Rice Flour, Water, Salt, Cumin Seeds
recipe_ingredients: Rice Flour, Water, Salt, Cumin Seeds
instructions: Boil water with a pinch of salt. Add rice flour and stir to form a smooth dough. Let it cool. Divide the dough into small balls and roll them into discs. Prepare a spicy chana filling using boiled chickpeas, spices, and onions. Place the filling in the center, fold into a triangular shape, and seal. Deep fry until golden brown. Serve with chutney.

available_ingredients: Rice, Curd, Green Chilies, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves, Turmeric
recipe_ingredients: Cooked Rice, Curd, Green Chilies, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves, Turmeric
instructions: Soak cooked rice in water overnight. Drain the water the next morning and mix the fermented rice with curd. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, green chilies, curry leaves, and turmeric. Pour the tempering over the rice and serve.

available_ingredients: Green Gram, Onions, Coriander, Salt, Oil
recipe_ingredients: Green Gram, Onions, Coriander, Salt, Oil
instructions: Soak green gram and grind into a smooth batter. Add chopped onions, coriander, and salt. Heat a tawa, pour a ladle of batter, and spread like a pancake. Cook both sides until golden. Serve with chutney or curd.

available_ingredients: Rice Flour, Salt, Cumin Seeds, Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice Flour, Salt, Cumin Seeds, Oil
instructions: Knead rice flour with salt, cumin seeds, and water to form a dough. Roll into thick discs and roast on a tawa until golden brown. Serve with ghee or chutney.

available_ingredients: Rice, Black Gram Lentils, Salt, Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice, Black Gram Lentils, Salt, Oil
instructions: Soak black gram lentils, grind into a thick paste, and mix with salt and chopped onions. Shape into small balls and steam until cooked. Serve with chutney.

available_ingredients: Rice, Black Gram Lentils, Turmeric, Mustard Seeds, Green Chilies, Salt, Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice, Black Gram Lentils, Turmeric, Mustard Seeds, Green Chilies, Salt, Oil
instructions: Wash and chop leafy greens like amaranth or spinach. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, green chilies, and lentils. Add chopped greens and turmeric. Stir-fry until tender. Season with salt and serve with rice.

available_ingredients: Rice, Tamarind, Jaggery, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves, Dry Red Chilies
recipe_ingredients: Rice, Tamarind, Jaggery, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves, Dry Red Chilies
instructions: Soak tamarind and extract its juice. Heat oil, add mustard seeds, dry red chilies, and curry leaves. Pour in tamarind juice, add jaggery, and bring to a boil. Simmer until thickened. Serve with steamed rice.

recipe_ingredients: Rice, Jaggery, Cardamom, Ghee
instructions: Grind soaked rice into a paste. Melt jaggery and mix it with the rice paste. Add cardamom powder. Heat ghee in a pan and cook the mixture until golden. Shape into small squares and serve.

available_ingredients: Rice Flour, Salt, Water, Cumin Seeds
recipe_ingredients: Rice Flour, Salt, Water, Cumin Seeds
instructions: Mix rice flour with water, salt, and cumin seeds. Knead into a stiff dough. Roll into thin strips and deep fry until crispy. Serve as a crunchy snack.

available_ingredients: Rice Flour, Curd, Green Chilies, Coriander, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves
recipe_ingredients: Rice Flour, Curd, Green Chilies, Coriander, Mustard Seeds, Curry Leaves
instructions: Mix rice flour with water to form a dough. Shape into small balls and steam them. Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and green chilies for tempering. Pour curd over the steamed balls and garnish with coriander. Serve chilled.

recipe_ingredients: Wheat Flour, Jaggery, Cardamom, Ghee
instructions: Mix wheat flour, grated jaggery, and cardamom powder. Knead into a soft dough. Shape into small discs and deep fry until golden brown. Serve as a sweet treat.

available_ingredients: Black Gram, Rice, Green Chilies, Ginger, Salt, Oil
recipe_ingredients: Black Gram, Rice, Green Chilies, Ginger, Salt, Oil
instructions: Mix wheat flour, grated jaggery, and cardamom powder. Knead into a soft dough. Shape into small discs and deep fry until golden brown. Serve as a sweet treat.
column5: Soak black gram and rice overnight. Grind into a thick batter. Add chopped green chilies, ginger, and salt. Heat oil in a pan, pour the batter in small portions, and fry until crispy. Serve with chutney.

available_ingredients: Rice, Lentils, Mustard Seeds, Ginger, Garlic, Salt, Oil
recipe_ingredients: Rice, Lentils, Mustard Seeds, Ginger, Garlic, Salt, Oil
instructions: Boil rice and lentils together. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, ginger, and garlic. Stir in the rice and lentil mixture. Cook for a few minutes and serve with chutney.

available_ingredients: Potatoes, Poppy Seeds, Mustard Oil, Green Chilies, Turmeric, Salt
recipe_ingredients: Potatoes, Poppy seed paste, Mustard oil, Green chilies, Turmeric, Salt
instructions: Peel and chop the potatoes into small cubes. Soak the poppy seeds in warm water for 30 minutes and grind them into a smooth paste. Heat mustard oil in a pan until it smokes lightly. Add green chilies for aroma. Add the chopped potatoes and turmeric powder, stirring well. Cook until the potatoes are half done. Then add the poppy seed paste and some water. Simmer until the potatoes are soft, and serve with rice.

available_ingredients: Hilsa Fish, Mustard Seeds, Mustard Oil, Green Chilies, Turmeric, Salt
recipe_ingredients: Hilsa fish, Mustard seed paste, Mustard oil, Green chilies, Turmeric, Salt
instructions: Clean and cut the fish into pieces. Rub the fish with turmeric and salt. Soak mustard seeds in water, then grind them into a fine paste. Heat mustard oil in a pan and fry the fish lightly. Remove the fish and add the mustard paste, green chilies, and some water. Let the sauce simmer, then add the fish and cook until done. Serve with steamed rice

available_ingredients: Mutton, Onions, Garlic, Ginger, Tomatoes, Spices, Mustard Oil
recipe_ingredients: Mutton, Onions, Garlic, Ginger, Tomatoes, Yogurt, Garam masala, Mustard oil
instructions: Marinate the mutton with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and chili powder for an hour. Heat mustard oil in a pot and sauté the onions until golden brown. Add the marinated mutton and stir until the meat is sealed. Add tomatoes, garam masala, and cook until the oil separates. Add water and cover the pot to cook the mutton until tender. Serve with rice or roti.

available_ingredients: Prawns, Coconut Milk, Mustard Oil, Ginger, Garlic, Spices
recipe_ingredients: Prawns, Coconut milk, Mustard oil, Ginger, Garlic, Turmeric, Garam masala
instructions: Clean and marinate the prawns with turmeric and salt. Heat mustard oil and fry the prawns lightly. Remove and set aside. In the same oil, add ginger and garlic paste and sauté until fragrant. Pour in the coconut milk and bring it to a gentle boil. Add garam masala and return the prawns to the pan. Simmer until the prawns are cooked through. Serve with steamed rice.

recipe_ingredients: Milk, Yogurt, Sugar
instructions: Boil the milk until it reduces slightly. Add sugar and stir until dissolved. Allow the milk to cool to lukewarm temperature. Mix in the yogurt and pour it into a clay pot or any container. Let it ferment in a warm place for 6-8 hours or until set. Chill before serving. Mishti Doi is best enjoyed cold.

available_ingredients: Eggplant, Mustard Oil, Green Chilies, Onions, Garlic, Salt
recipe_ingredients: Eggplant, Mustard oil, Green chilies, Onions, Garlic, Salt
instructions: Roast the eggplant over an open flame or bake it until the skin is charred and the flesh is soft. Peel off the skin and mash the pulp. Heat mustard oil in a pan and add chopped onions, green chilies, and garlic. Sauté until fragrant. Add the mashed eggplant and stir well. Season with salt and cook for a few minutes. Serve with rice.

available_ingredients: Khoya, Paneer, Flour, Baking Soda, Cardamom Powder, Sugar, Water
recipe_ingredients: Khoya, Paneer, Flour, Baking soda, Cardamom powder, Sugar, Water
instructions: Mix khoya, crumbled paneer, flour, and a pinch of baking soda. Knead into a smooth dough and form small round balls. Heat oil and fry the balls until golden brown. Prepare a sugar syrup with water, sugar, and cardamom powder. Add the fried Pantua to the syrup and let them soak for a few hours. Serve warm or chilled.

available_ingredients: Rice Flour, Jaggery, Coconut, Cardamom Powder
recipe_ingredients: Rice flour, Grated coconut, Jaggery, Cardamom powder, Water
instructions: Prepare a dough using rice flour and warm water. For the filling, cook grated coconut with jaggery until it thickens and add cardamom powder. Form small dumplings with the dough, stuff them with the filling, and seal. Steam or fry the dumplings and serve warm. Some varieties are soaked in thickened milk for added sweetness.

available_ingredients: Rohu Fish, Mustard Oil, Potatoes, Tomatoes, Ginger, Garlic, Turmeric, Spices
recipe_ingredients: Rohu fish, Mustard oil, Potatoes, Tomatoes, Ginger, Garlic, Turmeric, Cumin, Bay leaf
instructions: Clean the fish and marinate it with turmeric and salt. Fry the fish pieces in mustard oil until golden. Remove and set aside. In the same oil, sauté chopped garlic, ginger, and bay leaf. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add the potatoes, spices, and water to create a curry. Once the potatoes are cooked, add the fried fish pieces and simmer. Serve with rice.

recipe_ingredients: Bitter gourd, Eggplant, Potato, Drumstick, Raw banana, Mustard oil, Mustard seeds, Poppy seed paste, Milk, Panch phoron
instructions: Cut the vegetables into pieces. Heat mustard oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and panch phoron (Bengali five-spice mix). Add the bitter gourd first, followed by the other vegetables. Cook gently and add poppy seed paste for a creamy texture. Finish with milk for slight sweetness. Shukto is often served as the first course in a traditional Bengali meal.
